arguments.task: Visit official Salesforce developer documentation and official Salesforce pages to verify API authentication methods and surface. Find pages showing supported auth methods for Salesforce APIs, including OAuth and any mention of username-password or session token flows; verify whether API keys are supported for core Salesforce APIs. Also find official docs for Salesforce REST API and GraphQL API. Return concise extracted facts with exact source URLs only from Salesforce-owned domains.

input.thought: Start official-source browsing for auth and API surface evidence.

arguments.task: Visit official Salesforce pricing, help, or developer pages to verify whether API access is self-serve or requires paid plans/admin approval. Find official Salesforce pages that describe API access entitlement or limits by edition/plan, and any rate limit documentation for REST/GraphQL/core APIs. Return concise extracted facts with exact source URLs only from Salesforce-owned domains.

input.thought: Start official-source browsing for access tier and rate limit evidence.

arguments.task: Visit the official Salesforce GitHub organization and official Salesforce-owned sites to determine whether Salesforce has an official MCP server or MCP-related repository/documentation. Prefer first-party pages and official GitHub repos. Return concise extracted facts with exact source URLs only.

input.thought: Start official-source browsing for MCP evidence.

API Access Entitlement by Edition**\\n- **Developer Edition:** Built-in, self-serve access.\\n- **Enterprise, Unlimited, and Performance Editions:** Built-in access included in the plan.\\n- **Professional Edition:** Not included by default; requires a paid add-on or administrative activation to enable API access.\\n- **Essentials Edition:** Generally does not support API access (except for specific integrations like Mass Email or through certain partner apps).\\n\\n**2. Rate Limits & Allocations (REST, SOAP, GraphQL)**\\n- **Total API Request Limit (24-hour window):** Calculated as a base amount (e.g., 100,000 for Enterprise) + (number of licenses × calls per license) + any purchased add-ons.\\n  - *Salesforce/Platform License:* 1,000 calls (Enterprise), 5,000 calls (Unlimited/Performance).\\n- **Concurrent Request Limits:** \\n  - Production/Sandboxes: 25 concurrent requests (lasting 20s or longer).\\n  - Developer/Trial: 5 concurrent requests.\\n- **GraphQL API:** Uses the same limits as the **Connect RE...[truncated]

{"_meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{\"data\":{\"results\":[{\"response\":{\"successful\":true,\"data\":{\"current_goal\":\"\",\"current_step\":9,\"current_url\":\"https://developer.salesforce.com/blogs/2025/06/introducing-mcp-support-across-salesforce\",\"id\":\"26df6fd3-3ec3-4d7e-adfd-a162c33879e8\",\"isSuccess\":true,\"output\":\"Salesforce has officially embraced the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with several first-party implementations and developer tools. Below are the official MCP servers, repositories, and documentation as of June 2025:\\n\\n### Official Salesforce MCP Servers & Repositories\\n- **Salesforce DX MCP Server (Developer Preview)**: Enables development tasks (deploying code, creating scratch orgs) via natural language.\\n  - **Repo**: https://github.com/salesforcecli/mcp\\n  - **Blog Reference**: https://developer.salesforce.com/blogs/2025/06/level-up-your-developer-tools-with-salesforce-dx-mcp\\n- **Heroku Platform MCP Server (GA)**: Facilitates Heroku app management (dyno checks, add-ons) using natural language.\\n  - **Repo**: https://github.com/heroku/heroku-mcp-server\\n- **MuleSoft MCP Server (GA)**: Allows management and deployment of MuleSoft projects.\\n  - **NPM Package**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@mulesoft/mcp-server\\n  - **Official Announcement**: https://blogs.mulesoft.com/news/model-context-protocol/\\n\\n### Documentation and Official Sites\\n- **Official Salesforce Developers Blog (Main Announcement)**: \\\\\\\"Introd...[truncated]

raw_result.description: CRM platform with customer data, sales automation, and extensible APIs for platform and business objects.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Salesforce docs show OAuth 2.0 for connected apps and list OAuth authorization flows including username-password, client credentials, and JWT bearer flows. Core API calls also use Salesforce session/access tokens after auth. No first-party evidence was verified for API key auth for core Salesforce APIs from the gathered pages, so API key is not included. Username-password flow is classified under other because allowed enum does not include it directly.
raw_result.gating_reason: API access depends on Salesforce edition. Gathered official limits/access evidence indicates Enterprise, Unlimited, and Performance include API access, while Professional requires an add-on/activation and some lower tiers may not include API access by default.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API surface including REST APIs and GraphQL API, with platform/business object access plus Connect/Chatter-related APIs and documented limits pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Salesforce has official MCP support evidence from Salesforce Developers, including the Salesforce DX MCP Server repository under the Salesforce CLI GitHub org and official hosted MCP server documentation/blog material.

raw_result.blocker: Public docs and auth exist, but first toolkit build may require a paid Salesforce edition with API access enabled; edition-dependent entitlement creates a practical access workaround rather than fully self-serve readiness.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits docs indicate 24-hour API request allocations by edition/license, concurrent long-running request limits (25 for production/sandbox orgs and 5 for developer/trial orgs in gathered evidence), and GraphQL uses Connect REST API limits; Connect REST/Chatter also has per-user, per-application, per-hour limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official REST and GraphQL docs are public; Salesforce has first-party MCP momentum with Salesforce DX MCP Server and broader Salesforce MCP announcements; API entitlement is edition-sensitive rather than universally self-serve.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some conclusions rely on extracted summaries from browser runs because several Salesforce docs/help pages rendered inconsistently. API-key non-support for core APIs was inferred from absence in gathered first-party auth pages rather than a directly quoted denial, and exact edition/pricing entitlement details may vary by product/plan.

result.description: CRM platform with customer data, sales automation, and extensible APIs for platform and business objects.
result.auth_notes: Official Salesforce docs show OAuth 2.0 for connected apps and list OAuth authorization flows including username-password, client credentials, and JWT bearer flows. Core API calls also use Salesforce session/access tokens after auth. No first-party evidence was verified for API key auth for core Salesforce APIs from the gathered pages, so API key is not included. Username-password flow is classified under other because allowed enum does not include it directly.
result.gating_reason: API access depends on Salesforce edition. Gathered official limits/access evidence indicates Enterprise, Unlimited, and Performance include API access, while Professional requires an add-on/activation and some lower tiers may not include API access by default.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API surface including REST APIs and GraphQL API, with platform/business object access plus Connect/Chatter-related APIs and documented limits pages.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Salesforce has official MCP support evidence from Salesforce Developers, including the Salesforce DX MCP Server repository under the Salesforce CLI GitHub org and official hosted MCP server documentation/blog material.

result.blocker: Public docs and auth exist, but first toolkit build may require a paid Salesforce edition with API access enabled; edition-dependent entitlement creates a practical access workaround rather than fully self-serve readiness.
result.rate_limit_notes: Official limits docs indicate 24-hour API request allocations by edition/license, concurrent long-running request limits (25 for production/sandbox orgs and 5 for developer/trial orgs in gathered evidence), and GraphQL uses Connect REST API limits; Connect REST/Chatter also has per-user, per-application, per-hour limits.
result.notable_signals: Official REST and GraphQL docs are public; Salesforce has first-party MCP momentum with Salesforce DX MCP Server and broader Salesforce MCP announcements; API entitlement is edition-sensitive rather than universally self-serve.
result.human_review_reason: Some conclusions rely on extracted summaries from browser runs because several Salesforce docs/help pages rendered inconsistently. API-key non-support for core APIs was inferred from absence in gathered first-party auth pages rather than a directly quoted denial, and exact edition/pricing entitlement details may vary by product/plan.
